narrative: Dollar retreats as global equities rally and crude oil consolidates, driving DXY lower -0.28% while gold holds near $4,136 -0.10% on US-Iran conflict assessment. The risk-on tone lifts GBP/USD to 1.3446 and EUR/USD to 1.1461, supported by rising ECB and BoE rate hike bets that challenge the Fed's +150bp nominal carry advantage. USD/JPY falls to 161.446 as Japan's producer prices hit their fastest pace since 2023, keeping the BoJ on track to tighten; options markets show a moderate put skew with USD/JPY 25d RR at -1.28. Equities rise +0.81% and the VIX consolidates 15.8. Watch the BoC rate decision on July 15 as the next key policy catalyst.
